title: Connect External Gear
description: Learn how to integrate the EP-133 with other equipment including MIDI devices, Pocket Operators, and your computer.

steps.title: Connect to a Mixer or Speakers
steps.instruction: Connect the OUTPUT port to your mixer, audio interface, or powered speakers using a 3.5mm stereo cable.
steps.tip: Use the VOLUME knob to adjust the output level. Start low and increase gradually.

steps.title: Connect a MIDI Device
steps.instruction: For MIDI, you'll need a 3.5mm TRS to 5-pin DIN adapter (Type-A). Connect the EP-133's MIDI port to your synth or drum machine.

substeps.instruction: Use a Type-A TRS-to-DIN adapter cable
Connect to your synth's MIDI IN to trigger it from the EP-133
Or connect to MIDI OUT to control the EP-133 from a controller

steps.tip: Type-A is the MMA standard. Check your adapter - Type-B won't work correctly!

steps.title: Configure MIDI Settings
steps.instruction: Press SHIFT + ERASE to open the System menu. Navigate to MIDI settings to configure channels and clock behavior.

substeps.instruction: Set MIDI input channel (1-16 or Omni)
Enable MIDI Thru if chaining devices

steps.title: Connect a Pocket Operator
steps.instruction: Connect a Pocket Operator to the SYNC port using a standard 3.5mm stereo cable. The EP-133 sends sync on the right channel and receives audio on the left.
steps.tip: Set the EP-133 to PO-8 or PO-16 sync mode in the System menu.

steps.title: Connect to Your Computer
steps.instruction: Connect the USB-C port to your computer. The EP-133 appears as a class-compliant MIDI device - no drivers needed.

substeps.instruction: EP-133 appears as a MIDI device in your DAW
Enable USB as clock source if syncing to DAW
Record audio from OUTPUT to a separate audio input

steps.title: Set Clock Source
steps.instruction: In the System menu, set the clock source based on your setup: Internal (EP-133 is master), MIDI (external hardware is master), or USB (computer/DAW is master).
steps.tip: Only one device should be the clock master. All others should follow to stay in sync.

outcomes: Ability to integrate with MIDI devices and Pocket Operators
Knowledge of clock sync for multi-device setups
